meta.title: loadout v0.15.0 — learning (ambient harvest)
meta.goal_md: After a one-time `load learn on`, loadout mines the user's own agent-session transcripts for durable cross-project preferences and stages them as candidate fragments in an inbox; the user promotes or dismisses them in the studio. Harvesting runs as one-shot, bounded, logged invocations of an agent CLI the user already has — no daemon, no OS scheduler, no LLM call on any render path.
meta.summary_md: This release implements the approved `design-learning.md` (critic + codex gpt-5.5 approved; spec-review amendments folded; all plan-time gates resolved). The observable outcome: `load learn on` prints an honest consent (per-machine ceiling of ≤4 extraction calls/day at defaults, concrete cost estimate), registers session-end hooks for claude (SessionEnd, nested schema) and cursor (stop, flat schema), and from then on a throttled detached worker mines new transcripts past per-machine watermarks, stages candidates into per-machine append-only journals (sync-merge-clean), and surfaces them in a new studio Inbox tab plus one discovery line in run/refresh output. **Plan-time gate results baked in:** gemini hooks FAILED the gate (inert settings entries; gemini ships tier-2 readers-only); claude's interactive marker is the verified transcript `entrypoint` field; SessionEnd fires for `-p` runs too, so hook-named sessions get no exclusion exemption; no Windows dist target ships (spawn path is `#[cfg(unix)]` with graceful no-op). **Global constraints (every task):** run all cargo commands as `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo …`; branch `feat/learning` off `origin/main` @ `aa28149`; Conventional Commits with the noreply email, one commit per task; NO new crate dependencies (random bytes come from `/dev/urandom` via std, unix); machine-local state lives under `config::state_dir()` (never syncs), journals under the config dir `inbox/` (sync by design); no new serde keys on `deny_unknown_fields` structs — built-in-only descriptor data uses `#[serde(skip)]` (the `review_commands` precedent); render/launch/hook paths must never block on learning work; TDD — write the failing test first in every task. The ask: review task boundaries, the two medium-risk hook tasks, and the three open questions (none blocking).
key_points: **Foundations first** — `[learn]` config table (global-only, stripped from repo layers), per-machine identity + activation ack + two stamps in the state dir, append-only inbox journals with fold/dedupe/suppression permanence, versioned watermarks with monotonic advance, and an O_EXCL lock whose fencing token gates every later side effect.

**Readers are deterministic and fail closed per store** — claude (offset-resumed JSONL, `entrypoint` ∈ {cli, claude-desktop} only), codex (`session_meta.originator/source` gate), gemini (rewritten-array store, processed-session set, single-message heuristic). User-authored messages only, redacted before anything leaves the process.

**One extraction call per tick** — spend stamp written immediately BEFORE the call (crash-loop ceiling holds), scan stamp bounds thrash, ≤20 slices / ≤400KB newest-first with over-cap content dropped-not-deferred, pending-claim anchoring capped at the 50 most-observed, strict JSON validation, claim gate (redact + injection lint + length cap → quarantine).

**Triggers are clock ticks** — claude SessionEnd + cursor stop hooks and cheap checks at run/refresh/studio/hook entry points all funnel into `learn::maybe_spawn`, which double-spawns one detached, niced, deadline-bounded worker. Gemini hooks are OUT (gate failed — inert settings entries).

**Hook machinery grows, carefully** — `HookRegistry` gains code-side-only purpose ids (freshness vs learn) and a format discriminator (cursor flat vs claude nested writer); passive bootstrap can never re-add learn hooks after `load learn off`; removal never touches freshness or foreign entries; timestamped backups; `disableAllHooks` respected.

**Surfaces** — `load learn on|off|status|reset` + `load harvest`; studio Inbox tab (promote via the existing Session stage/apply engine with a pending-dispositions flush mirroring `pending_trust`; dismiss = permanent suppression; quarantine held for edit); doctor learn section + orphaned-hooks flag; one discovery line in run/refresh when candidates are pending.

**Spend honesty end-to-end** — consent text names the per-machine ceiling, the exact hook files edited, that claim text (not quotes) syncs, and a concrete per-run cost estimate; every run is logged and attributable; 2 consecutive failures pause ambient triggering.

**Ship gate** — stub-CLI end-to-end integration suite (full cycle, no-op run costs zero calls, crash-after-stamp burns only the tick, recursion guard, reclaim-as-failure), full suite + clippy + fmt green, CHANGELOG task included (the v0.13 gap).
out_of_scope: The audit layer (loadout-audit) — deferred to v0.16 per the critic-driven cut
OS-scheduled triggering (rejected; the worker keeps a trigger-agnostic --ambient entry)
Gemini/copilot/codex/opencode hook registration (gemini gate failed; others tier-2/deferred)
Windows detach support (no Windows dist target ships; source builds degrade gracefully)
Journal compaction (rule pre-committed: dispositions preserved forever)
Similarity-based suppression and prompt-side suppression hints (paraphrase resurfacing accepted for v0.15)
Auto-promotion, assistant-message mining, cross-person aggregation (teams track)

phases.title: Foundations — pure logic, fully unit-testable
phases.summary_md: The stores and primitives everything else writes to or reads from. No triggers, no LLM, no hooks yet. Each task is standalone and lands with its own tests.

tasks.title: Branch + [learn] config table (global-only, forward-compatible)
tasks.summary_md:
Create `feat/learning` off `origin/main` (`git fetch && git switch -c feat/learning origin/main` — main is `aa28149`). Then add the `[learn]` table mirroring `[sync]`'s five touchpoints in `src/config.rs`.

**Produces:**
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
pub struct LearnConfig {
    pub enabled: bool,              // default false
    pub interval: Duration,         // default 6h, parsed from "6h" via providers::parse_duration
    pub scope: LearnScope,          // Adopted (default) | All
    pub cli: Option<String>,        // pin, e.g. "claude"
    pub model: Option<String>,      // pin, e.g. "haiku"
}
pub enum LearnScope { Adopted, All }
```
plus `learn: LearnConfig` on `Config` (src/config.rs:31-68).

**Steps:** (1) failing tests: defaults resolve (disabled, 6h, adopted); `[learn] enabled = true` parses; later layer wins on merge; unknown `[learn]` subkey warns but doesn't fail (forward-compat — RawLearn is `#[serde(default)]`, NOT deny_unknown_fields, matching the settings-struct convention at config.rs:334-341); a REPO-layer `[learn]` is stripped (`strip_global_only`, config.rs:296-330 — add `parsed.learn = None;` beside the `[sync]` strip at :316-319, so a cloned repo can never toggle learning). (2) Implement: `RawLearn` + field on `RawConfig` (:343-372); `"learn"` in `KNOWN_TOP_LEVEL` (:405-419) and the sub-table list in `warn_unknown_config_keys` (:438-446); merge block copying `[sync]` (:508-522); finalize (:572-632). (3) `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test config::` green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): add [learn] config table (global-only, forward-compatible)`.
acceptance: bare config resolves learn.enabled=false, interval=6h, scope=adopted
repo-layer [learn] is stripped; global layers merge later-wins
unknown [learn] subkeys warn, never fail (newer-config forward-compat)

tasks.title: Machine identity, activation ack, stamps, failure counter
acceptance: machine id minted exactly once, stable across calls, 32 hex chars
is_due handles never-ran, elapsed, and clock-went-backwards (never stuck)
2 consecutive failures => paused; reset clears

tasks.title: Inbox journal store — append, fold, dedupe, suppression permanence
acceptance: same-claim events from two machine journals fold to one candidate with accumulated distinct-session count
dismissed candidate never returns to pending on later observations; unsuppress does
malformed lines tolerated; quarantined candidates never fold to pending

tasks.title: Versioned watermark store with monotonic advance
acceptance: offsets can only grow; regression attempts are ignored
a newer-versioned file is refused loudly (corrupt semantics), never misread or silently reset
save prunes marks for files that no longer exist

tasks.title: O_EXCL lock with fencing token and stale reclaim
acceptance: second acquire against a live holder is Busy; against dead-pid or 2×deadline-age is Reclaimed
still_held() is false the moment the file carries a different token; release never deletes a foreign lock

phases.title: Transcript capture — deterministic, fail-closed readers
phases.summary_md: Per-store readers extract user-authored messages from interactive sessions only, past the watermarks. A store that fails to parse is skipped with a log line — readers fail closed per store, never abort the run. Fixtures are cut from real (sanitized) transcript files.

tasks.title: Reader framework + claude reader (entrypoint-gated, offset-resumed)
tasks.summary_md:
**Produces (src/learn/readers/mod.rs):**
```rust
pub struct SessionSlice { pub agent: &'static str, pub session_id: String, pub cwd: Option<PathBuf>,
    pub ts: String /* newest message ts */, pub messages: Vec<String> /* user-authored, in order */,
    pub source_file: PathBuf, pub end_offset: u64 /* byte offset consumed, for watermark advance */ }
pub struct ScanOutcome { pub slices: Vec<SessionSlice>, pub skipped_stores: Vec<String> /* "claude: <why>" */ }
pub const QUIESCENCE: Duration = Duration::from_secs(20 * 60);
```
**src/learn/readers/claude.rs:**
```rust
pub fn scan_claude(home: &Path, marks: &Watermarks, now: SystemTime, hooked: &BTreeSet<String>) -> Vec<SessionSlice>
```
Walks `home/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl`. Per file: skip when `mtime` younger than QUIESCENCE unless its session_id ∈ `hooked` (hook-named sessions bypass quiescence but NOT the exclusions below); resume from `marks.mark(path).bytes_processed`; parse lines leniently (bad line → skip line). Include a message line iff `type == "user"` AND `userType == "external"` AND not `isMeta` AND not `isSidechain`; message content is `message.content` when string, else joins text parts. **Session-level exclusion (verified plan-time):** any line in the file carrying `entrypoint` outside {`cli`, `claude-desktop`} excludes the whole file (`sdk-cli`/`sdk-ts` are programmatic). `cwd` from the first line that has one. `end_offset` = byte offset after the last fully-parsed line.

**Steps:** (1) Build fixtures under `tests/fixtures/learn/claude/`: interactive file (entrypoint cli, 3 user messages + assistant noise), sdk file (entrypoint sdk-cli), sidechain file, file with a malformed line mid-stream, file to test offset resume. Failing unit tests in-module reading fixtures via a `home`-shaped tempdir. (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): claude transcript reader (interactive-only, offset-resumed)`.
acceptance: sdk-cli/sdk-ts sessions and sidechain files are excluded whole; cli and claude-desktop included
resume from a recorded offset re-reads nothing; malformed mid-file lines skip that line only
files younger than 20min are skipped unless hook-named

tasks.title: codex reader (session_meta-gated)
tasks.summary_md:
**Produces (src/learn/readers/codex.rs):** `pub fn scan_codex(home: &Path, marks: &Watermarks, now: SystemTime) -> Vec<SessionSlice>` over `home/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl`.

First line is `{type: "session_meta", payload: {id, cwd, originator, source, cli_version, …}}` (verified on real files). **Include the file only when `originator == "codex_cli_rs"` AND `source == "cli"`** — exec/programmatic sessions carry different values and are excluded. `cwd` and `session_id` come from the meta payload.

**First implementation step (in-task verification):** the user-message line shape inside rollouts was NOT verified at plan time — before writing fixtures, inspect a real rollout on this machine (`head -40 $(ls -t ~/.codex/sessions/*/*/*/rollout-*.jsonl | head -1)`), identify the user-message record type and its text field, and encode EXACTLY that shape into `tests/fixtures/learn/codex/`. If user text turns out not to be recoverable from rollouts, the reader returns empty and logs `skipped_stores` — fail closed, do not guess.

Offset resume identical to claude (append-only JSONL, `marks` keyed by path). Quiescence identical (no codex hooks in v0.15, so no `hooked` bypass).

**Steps:** (1) verify line shape as above; (2) failing tests on fixtures: interactive included, exec-source excluded, offset resume, malformed line tolerated; (3) implement; (4) green; (5) commit `feat(learn): codex transcript reader (session_meta-gated)`.
acceptance: exec-mode sessions (originator/source ≠ cli values) are excluded whole
fixtures match the REAL rollout line shape captured from this machine, not an invented one

tasks.title: gemini reader (rewritten-array store, session-set watermarks)
tasks.summary_md:
**Produces (src/learn/readers/gemini.rs):** `pub fn scan_gemini(home: &Path, marks: &Watermarks, now: SystemTime, work_dir_hash: &str) -> Vec<SessionSlice>` over `home/.gemini/tmp/<project-hash>/logs.json`.

`logs.json` is a rewritten ARRAY of `{sessionId, messageId, type: "user", message, timestamp}` (verified) — no byte offsets possible, so eligibility is the processed-`sessionId` set (`marks.gemini_seen/record`). Group entries by `sessionId`; a session is eligible when its newest timestamp is older than QUIESCENCE and it isn't already processed. **Programmatic exclusion (fallback heuristic per Decision #6):** skip sessions with exactly one user message — `-p` one-shots look exactly like that; gemini has no verified marker. **cwd guard:** the `<project-hash>` dir whose hash equals `work_dir_hash` (the worker's own work dir) is dropped entirely — this covers gemini's missing no-persist flag.

**In-task verification step:** confirm the project-hash formula by hashing a known repo path (sha256 of the absolute cwd string is the expected formula) and comparing against an actual `~/.gemini/tmp/` dir name for a repo with known gemini use; encode the confirmed formula as `pub fn gemini_project_hash(path: &Path) -> String`. If the formula doesn't match, scope=adopted simply can't map gemini sessions to repos — then gemini sessions are only included under scope=all, and the reader documents that (fail closed, log line).

**Steps:** (1) verification above; (2) failing tests: multi-session file folds correctly, single-message session excluded, processed set skips, work-dir hash dropped; (3) implement; (4) green; (5) commit `feat(learn): gemini reader (session-set watermarks, single-message heuristic)`.
acceptance: single-user-message sessions are excluded (programmatic heuristic)
processed sessionIds never re-harvest; the worker's own project-hash dir is always dropped

tasks.title: Scope filter, caps, redaction, sentinel — slice assembly
acceptance: caps enforce ≤20 sessions and ≤400KB, newest-first, with dropped counts reported
secrets are redacted before anything is size-counted or returned
adopted-scope keeps only sessions in adopted repos; sentinel and work-dir sessions always dropped

phases.title: Extraction — prompt, claim gate, agent-CLI runner
phases.summary_md: The one LLM touchpoint, fenced on all sides: deterministic prompt build, strict output validation, deterministic defenses on every claim before it can sync or re-enter a prompt.

tasks.title: Extraction prompt + strict output validation + pending-claim anchoring
acceptance: pending-claim anchoring is capped at the 50 most-observed
malformed or extra-field output is an Err, never partially accepted
re-observed verbatim claims produce identical candidate ids

tasks.title: Claim gate — redact + injection lint + caps → quarantine
tasks.summary_md:
Claims sync and re-enter future prompts, so they get the same deterministic defenses as rendered guidance (codex finding 6).

**Produces (src/learn/gate.rs):**
```rust
pub enum Gated { Clean(String) /* redacted claim */, Quarantined { claim: String, labels: Vec<&'static str> } }
pub fn gate_claim(claim: &str) -> Gated;      // redact::redact_secrets_report (src/redact.rs:58) then lint::find_injection (src/lint.rs:115); non-empty labels => Quarantined; then 500-char cap (truncate Clean, keep Quarantined whole for display)
pub fn gate_quote(quote: &str) -> String;     // redact + hard-truncate to 200 chars; caller caps at 5 quotes per candidate
```
Quarantined claims are still written to the journal (as `Observed` with `quarantined: Some(labels)`) so the studio can show "held by injection lint" with the matched pattern — but they are NEVER anchored into future prompts (enforced here: the worker builds `PendingClaim`s only from `Gated::Clean` candidates) and are promotable only after the user edits them (studio task).

**Steps:** (1) failing tests: one input per injection-lint class from the src/lint.rs corpus quarantines with the right label; a claim embedding a GitHub token comes out Clean with `***REDACTED***`; 600-char clean claim truncates to 500; clean text passes through. (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): deterministic claim gate with quarantine`.
acceptance: every injection-lint class quarantines with its human label; quarantined claims are never anchored
secrets in claims and quotes are redacted before any write

tasks.title: One-shot agent-CLI selection and bounded invocation
tasks.summary_md:
**Produces (src/learn/agent_cli.rs):**
```rust
pub struct CliChoice { pub cli_id: &'static str /* claude|codex|gemini */, pub program: String, pub model: String }
pub fn select(learn: &LearnConfig) -> Option<CliChoice>;   // learn.cli pin else probe order claude→codex→gemini via providers::probe_cli (src/providers/mod.rs:671, 3s-bounded)
pub fn invoke(choice: &CliChoice, prompt: &str, work_dir: &Path, deadline: Duration) -> anyhow::Result<InvokeOut>;
pub struct InvokeOut { pub text: String /* unwrapped model output */, pub usage: Option<String> /* raw usage blob when the CLI reports one */ }
```
Hygiene argv (spellings verified plan-time on installed binaries):
- claude: `-p --bare --no-session-persistence --output-format json --model <model>` + tool restriction — **pin down at implementation** whether `--disallowed-tools "*"` or `--allowedTools ""` actually blocks all tools (run the real binary once with a trivial prompt); `--bare` is doc-verified to skip ALL hooks. Unwrap: parse stdout JSON, take `.result`.
- codex: `exec -s read-only --ephemeral --output-schema <work_dir>/schema.json -m <model>` (write `extract::output_json_schema()` to that file first). Unwrap: verify against the stub AND one real run — codex's final-message location in `--json` output is pinned here.
- gemini: `-p --approval-mode plan -o json -m <model>`. Unwrap: parse stdout JSON `.response` (verify at implementation).
Prompt is passed on **stdin** (avoids argv limits). Invocation: `Command` with `cwd=work_dir`, env `LOADOUT_LEARN_WORKER=1`, run under `providers::output_with_timeout` (src/providers/mod.rs:530 — process-group + deadline-bounded pipe drain). Default models: claude `haiku`, gemini a flash-class id, codex its configured default — exact strings pinned at implementation against each CLI's model list (do NOT trust memory for model ids).

**Steps:** (1) failing tests using the `agent_stub` pattern (tests/cli.rs:873): stub `claude`/`codex`/`gemini` scripts that dump argv+env to a file and emit canned wrapped JSON; assert flags, env guard, stdin prompt delivery, unwrap per CLI; timeout kill via a sleeping stub. (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): one-shot agent CLI runner with hygiene flags`.
acceptance: probe order claude→codex→gemini with learn.cli override; missing CLIs skipped
every invocation carries LOADOUT_LEARN_WORKER=1, the hygiene flags, and the deadline bound
per-CLI output unwrapping proven against stubs (and one real-binary spot check for claude)

phases.title: Worker + triggers
phases.summary_md: The 8-step worker with the fencing token gating every side effect, the trigger fast path, the detached double-spawn, and the entry-point wiring.

tasks.title: Harvest worker orchestration + `load harvest`
tasks.summary_md:
**Produces (src/learn/worker.rs):** `pub fn run_harvest(cfg: &Config, manual: bool) -> anyhow::Result<RunOutcome>` implementing the design's 8 steps; `src/commands/harvest.rs` `run(rt, args)` wrapping it; cli.rs `Command::Harvest(HarvestArgs)` with hidden `--ambient` flag (`#[command(hide = true)]` stays visible as `load harvest` for manual runs; `--ambient` marks throttled ambient runs in the log).

Step order with fencing — EVERY numbered side effect first checks `guard.still_held()` and aborts (logged, counted as failure) if not:
1. `lock::acquire_at` (Reclaimed ⇒ `state::record_failure_at` immediately, then continue); Busy ⇒ exit quietly.
2. write scan stamp.
3. read watermarks (corrupt ⇒ loud warn naming `load learn reset`, abort as failure); scan all three readers; `slices::assemble`. Empty ⇒ append a no-op log entry, release, exit — **spend stamp untouched** (two-stamp semantics).
4-5. (inside assemble: extraction + redaction already applied).
6. write SPEND stamp, then ONE `agent_cli::invoke`. Manual runs (`load harvest`) skip the interval check upstream but still write the spend stamp here (a manual run resets the tick — cheapest honest semantics).
7. `extract::parse_output`; malformed ⇒ failed run: log, do NOT advance watermarks, do NOT write events (the spend stamp already burned the tick — by design). Valid ⇒ `gate::gate_claim` each; drop exact-duplicates of existing fragment descriptions; fold into `journal::Event`s (reusing anchored ids); quotes through `gate_quote` (≤5×200 chars) into `state_dir/learn/evidence/<id>.json`.
8. advance watermarks past EVERYTHING scanned (drop-don't-defer) + prune; append run log entry to `state_dir/learn/log.jsonl` `{ts, trigger, cli, model, sessions, dropped_over_cap, candidates, quarantined, duration_ms, outcome, usage}`; on success `reset_failures_at`; release lock.
Internal wall-clock deadline 5min checked between steps; the CLI call additionally bounded by `output_with_timeout`. Work dir: `state_dir/learn/work/` (created; cwd for the CLI call; schema.json written here).

**Steps:** (1) failing tests with stub CLI: full-cycle writes journal+evidence+log+marks; empty scan makes ZERO CLI invocations and leaves the spend stamp untouched; malformed stub output ⇒ failure logged, watermarks unmoved, spend stamp written; a guard whose lock file was overwritten aborts before the CLI spawn (token fence). (2) Implement + wire cli.rs/main.rs/commands/mod.rs. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): fenced harvest worker + load harvest`.
acceptance: empty scan: zero CLI calls, spend stamp untouched, no-op logged
spend stamp always precedes the CLI call; malformed output burns the tick and nothing else
a fenced-out worker (foreign token) makes no spend and no writes
every run appends one attributable log entry

tasks.title: Trigger fast path + detached double-spawn
tasks.summary_md:
**Produces (src/learn/trigger.rs):**
```rust
pub enum Trigger { Run, Refresh, Studio, HookServe, SessionEnd { agent: String } }
pub fn maybe_spawn(cfg: &Config, trigger: Trigger);   // never blocks, never errors outward
```
Guards in order (each a cheap early return; residual idle cost = one config field + two stat()s): `cfg.learn.enabled` (config is already loaded at every site, read AFTER the flow's auto-pull); activation ack present; `LOADOUT_LEARN != "off"`; `LOADOUT_LEARN_WORKER` unset; not `paused_at`; scan stamp due (15min); spend stamp due (`learn.interval`) OR an eligibility hint exists (`state_dir/learn/eligible/` non-empty — written by session-end hooks). Then detached double-spawn (unix): intermediate `current_exe() harvest --ambient` child that itself spawns the real worker and exits; parent waits ONLY on the intermediate (ms). Worker child: `.process_group(0)` (the src/providers/mod.rs:543 precedent), stdio to `state_dir/learn/worker.log` (append), `nice` via `libc::nice(10)` in a `pre_exec` hook. `#[cfg(not(unix))]`: no-op (graceful — no Windows dist target ships).

Eligibility hints: `pub fn write_eligibility_hint(agent: &str, session_id: &str)` creates `state_dir/learn/eligible/<agent>-<session_id>`; the worker consumes (reads into the readers' `hooked` set, then deletes) at step 3.

**Steps:** (1) failing tests (env isolation via LOADOUT_STATE_DIR/LOADOUT_CONFIG_DIR tempdirs): each guard short-circuits — disabled config, missing ack, LOADOUT_LEARN=off, LOADOUT_LEARN_WORKER=1, fresh scan stamp, fresh spend stamp without hint; hint bypasses the spend-stamp guard but not the scan-stamp debounce. Spawn tested with the binary pointed at a stub via test seam `fn spawn_worker(exe: &Path)` injected. (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): trigger fast path + detached worker spawn`.
acceptance: all seven guards short-circuit in order; idle cost is a config read plus stamp stats
trigger latency is milliseconds (waits only on the intermediate); worker is reparented and group-isolated
non-unix builds compile to a graceful no-op

tasks.title: Entry-point wiring + discovery lines
tasks.summary_md:
Wire `maybe_spawn` into the four entry points and add the two user-visible lines.

**Wiring:** `src/commands/run.rs` — after `print_workflow_step` (:261), before `launch()` (:299): `learn::trigger::maybe_spawn(&prep.config, Trigger::Run)` (MUST complete its ms-wait before the `exec()` at :301 replaces the process — the double-spawn guarantees the worker survives). `src/commands/refresh.rs` — after `apply_for_agents` (:61). `src/studio/server.rs` — in `serve` after `bootstrap_hook_registrations` (:1785). `src/commands/hook.rs` — at the end of `serve` (still stdout-silent). All four sites run AFTER their flow's `sync_before_render`, so a synced disable lands before the check (Decision #4).

**Discovery lines:**
1. `load run` step line via `apply::step` (src/commands/apply.rs:110), emitted only when `journal::fold_at` has pending candidates: `✦ learn   3 staged suggestions await review — load studio`. Fold is a small file read; guard it behind `learn.enabled` so disabled users pay nothing.
2. Snapshot line in the rendered header (`src/render/header.rs:59` block): one line, only when N>0: `loadout: 3 staged suggestions await review — load studio`. CONSTRAINT: the header is composed with the context hash as an input, so this line must not feed the hash — assert in a test that two renders differing only in pending-count produce the SAME `context sha` in the header.
3. Refresh summary line: when `state_dir/learn/log.jsonl` has an ambient run newer than a `state_dir/learn/last-notified` stamp, refresh prints `learning: harvested 3 sessions via claude (haiku) — 2 new candidates` (then bumps the stamp).

**Steps:** (1) failing integration tests (tests/cli.rs Fixture): seeded journal ⇒ run/refresh show the line; empty ⇒ absent; hash-stability test for the header. (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): entry-point triggers + discovery lines`.
acceptance: all four entry points trigger; none can block or fail its host flow
discovery lines appear only when N>0; the header line never changes the recorded context sha
refresh summarizes new ambient runs exactly once
validation: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test --test cli learn

phases.title: Hook registration — claude nested + cursor stop
phases.summary_md: HookRegistry grows a code-side list with purpose ids so the passive bootstrap can never re-add learn hooks after `load learn off`, and removal never touches freshness or foreign entries. Gemini is OUT (gate failed).

tasks.title: HookRegistry: purpose ids, format discriminator, learn gating
acceptance: no new serde-visible config keys (all additions #[serde(skip)])
refresh bootstrap can never re-add learn hooks while learning is off
learn removal leaves freshness hooks and foreign entries untouched

tasks.title: Claude nested settings.json hook writer
tasks.summary_md:
**Create src/adapters/hooks_claude.rs** (registered from adapters/mod.rs; `apply_hook_registry` (:1140) dispatches on `HookFormat`). Target shape (doc-verified):
```json
{ "hooks": { "SessionEnd": [ { "hooks": [ { "type": "command", "command": "\"/path/to/load\" hook claude --event session-end", "timeout": 10 } ] } ] } }
```
No matcher (fires for all end reasons). `timeout: 10` — SessionEnd's default is 600s; ours only runs the fast path + ms spawn.

```rust
pub fn upsert_claude_hook(existing: &str, event: &str, subcommand: &str, command: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<String>>;
pub fn remove_claude_hook(existing: &str, subcommand: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Option<String>>;
```
Semantics mirror `upsert_hook_command` (:1204): identify ours by the `" <subcommand>"` command suffix; repoint a moved binary in place; `Ok(None)` when already current (no churn). **Semantic preservation, not byte preservation** (codex finding 7): serde_json re-serialization must keep every foreign key/value; loadout-created empty containers (`hooks.SessionEnd` array, matcher group, `hooks` object) are removed on deregistration when we emptied them; the shared one-time `.loadout-bak` backup flow (:1176) applies before first edit of a pre-existing file. **Respect `disableAllHooks: true`** (top-level key, doc-verified): register nothing, return a note string the caller surfaces ("claude hooks disabled by disableAllHooks — learning will rely on entry-point triggers").

**Steps:** (1) failing round-trip tests: upsert into an empty file, into a settings.json dense with foreign keys (assert every foreign key survives byte-for-byte at the VALUE level), repoint moved binary, idempotent second upsert = None, remove cleans empty containers but leaves a foreign SessionEnd sibling, disableAllHooks short-circuits. (2) Implement + dispatch in `apply_hook_registry`. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): claude nested hooks writer with semantic preservation`.
acceptance: foreign keys and sibling hook entries survive every upsert/remove round-trip
deregistration removes loadout-created empty containers, backup written before first edit
disableAllHooks means register nothing, with a user-visible note

tasks.title: `load hook <agent> --event session-end` serve path
tasks.summary_md:
**Modify src/cli.rs `HookArgs` (:113-120):** add `#[arg(long)] pub event: Option<String>`. **Modify src/commands/hook.rs:** when `event == Some("session-end")`, `serve` takes a different branch that keeps EVERY existing invariant (quiet warnings :58, stdin bounded 1MB :61, zero stdout, always exit 0): parse the payload leniently; for claude read `session_id` (payload also carries `transcript_path`, `cwd`, `reason` — doc-verified; `reason` is NOT used for exclusion, the reader's entrypoint filter is authoritative because SessionEnd fires for `-p` runs too); write the eligibility hint via `trigger::write_eligibility_hint(agent, session_id)`; call `maybe_spawn(cfg, Trigger::SessionEnd { agent })`; exit 0. No refresh work on this path.

**Cursor stop payload — in-task verification:** the brainstorm verified cursor has a session-end-class `stop` hook, but its payload fields were not re-verified at plan time. First step: check Cursor's hooks documentation for the `stop` event's payload (field for a conversation/session id). If a usable id exists, hint with it; if not, write a generic hint (`cursor-tick-<now>`) — the worker treats any hint as "a session just ended somewhere", scan-with-quiescence still applies to cursor since we ship no cursor reader in v0.15 (the hint's value for cursor is purely waking the claude/codex/gemini scan). If the docs show no `stop` event at all, drop the cursor learn hook from t-hook-registry's built-ins (tier-2 like gemini) and note it — do not guess.

**Steps:** (1) failing integration tests (tests/cli.rs): `load hook claude --event session-end` with a canned payload writes the hint file, prints NOTHING to stdout, exits 0; malformed/empty payload still exits 0; without `--event` the existing freshness behavior is unchanged (regression). (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): session-end hook serve path`.
acceptance: serve invariants hold: stdout-silent, exit 0 always, bounded stdin
claude payload marks its session eligible; freshness path is regression-free
cursor stop payload verified against docs or the hook is dropped to tier-2 — no guessing
validation: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test --test cli hook

phases.title: Surfaces — CLI, studio Inbox, doctor
phases.summary_md: Everything the user sees: consent and lifecycle commands, the review UI, and diagnostics.

tasks.title: `load learn on|off|status|reset` with honest consent
tasks.summary_md:
**Create src/commands/learn.rs; modify cli.rs/main.rs/commands/mod.rs.** `LearnArgs { action: Option<LearnAction> }`, `enum LearnAction { On { #[arg(long)] yes: bool }, Off, Status, Reset }`; bare `load learn` = status (the plan.rs:62 pattern).

**`on`:** print the consent block, then y/N stdin confirm (`--yes` skips; non-TTY without --yes aborts with a hint). Consent text MUST name, verbatim: what runs (`load harvest --ambient`, visible in ps), when (after agent sessions end and at loadout's own commands, at most once per 6h), the ceiling ("at most 1 extraction call per 6h tick — ≤4/day at defaults, per machine you enable; plus any `load harvest` you run yourself"), which files get edited (`~/.claude/settings.json`, `~/.cursor/hooks.json` — with one-time backups), the sync disclosure ("claim text distilled from your sessions is stored in your synced loadout config; verbatim quotes never leave this machine"), and the cost line — compute at implementation from the caps against CURRENT haiku-class API pricing (~100k input tokens worst case; verify pricing, do not trust memory; expected shape: "typically ~1-3¢, up to ~10-15¢ per run on a metered API key; $0 marginal on a subscription-backed CLI; pin `learn.cli` if that matters"). Then: set `enabled = true` in the GLOBAL config.toml via toml_edit (comment-preserving — the binding-writer precedent, NOT plain toml); mint machine id + write activation ack; register learn hooks (bootstrap with learn_active=true) listing each file touched; offer an immediate first run (y/N ⇒ `worker::run_harvest(manual)` foreground with its outcome printed).
**`off`:** set `enabled = false` (toml_edit), attempt a bounded sync auto-push when `sync::is_synced` (warn-don't-fail: "disable syncs to other machines at their next launch"), remove activation ack, `remove_learn_hooks` locally, print each file cleaned.
**`status`:** enabled/activated (machine id + hostname), CLI+model that would be selected, last run from log.jsonl, next eligibility (both stamps), paused state with clearing action, hooks registered per agent, journal counts (pending/suppressed/quarantined).
**`reset`:** `Watermarks::reset` + `reset_failures_at`; journals and evidence untouched; prints what re-baselining means (14-day cutoff forward).

**Steps:** (1) failing integration tests (Fixture, isolated HOME/CONFIG/STATE): `learn on --yes` writes enabled=true preserving config comments, mints id, writes ack, registers hooks in both dotfiles; `learn off` reverses all of it and flips the flag; `status` names paused state after seeded failures; `reset` clears watermarks only. (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): load learn lifecycle commands with honest consent`.
acceptance: consent names the per-machine ceiling, both hook files, the claim-sync disclosure, and a concrete cost estimate
on: config flag + ack + hooks, comments preserved; off: full local reversal + synced disable with push attempt
status and reset behave as designed (reset never touches journals)
validation: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test --test cli learn

tasks.title: Studio Inbox tab — review, promote, dismiss, history
tasks.summary_md:
Third top-level nav destination: **Loadouts | Library | Inbox** (with a pending-count badge when N>0). New module `src/studio/inbox.rs` for handlers+views (server.rs/views.rs are huge; only wiring edits go there).

**Routes (add to `route`'s match, server.rs:138; mutating routes are POST so they inherit the Origin/Referer guards):** `GET /tab/inbox` (candidate list; folds journals + machine-local evidence store OUTSIDE the state mutex, per the snapshot-then-render rule); `GET /inbox/<id>/promote` (modal: claim text editable — REQUIRED edit for quarantined; radio new-fragment vs merge-into-existing (fragment picker); profile multi-select); `POST /inbox/<id>/promote`; `POST /inbox/<id>/dismiss`; `POST /inbox/<id>/unsuppress`; `GET /inbox/history` (run-log panel folding `state_dir/learn/log.jsonl`: ts, trigger, cli/model, sessions, candidates, duration, outcome, usage).

**Promote plumbing:** stage the config edit through the existing `edit::Session` engine (`StagedOp::CreateFragment`/`EditFragment` + profile ref updates via existing ops — reuse `fragment_from_form`), and mirror the `pending_trust` precedent with `session.pending_dispositions: Vec<journal::Disposition>` flushed to the journal in `Session::apply()` — the disposition lands iff the config write lands. Dismiss/unsuppress append their disposition immediately (no config change involved). Editing a claim before promote changes its candidate id — the promote handler appends the disposition for the ORIGINAL id (that's what suppression/promotion must key on).

**Views:** candidate cards — claim, source badge, observation count, machines, evidence quotes (from the local evidence store; "evidence recorded on <host>" when only remote machines observed it), quarantine banner naming the matched lint pattern; suppressions list with un-suppress; empty states. Modal errors via `Resp::html_retarget` (the workflow-editor pattern).

**Steps:** (1) failing router unit tests via `state_for`/`req` (server.rs:2149/2179) with seeded tempdir journals: list renders candidates + badge count; promote stages a fragment op AND flushes the disposition on apply (assert config.toml + journal); dismiss folds to suppressed; quarantined candidate's promote form demands an edit; history renders seeded log lines. (2) Implement (+ minimal CSS in studio.css). (3) Green incl. `cargo test --test studio`. (4) Commit `feat(learn): studio inbox — review, promote, dismiss, history`.
acceptance: promote writes config through Session.apply and the disposition lands iff the write lands
dismissed candidates fold to suppressed and disappear from pending; unsuppress restores
quarantined claims are promotable only after user edit; evidence locality is honest per machine

tasks.title: Doctor learn section + refresh pause warning
tasks.summary_md:
**Modify src/commands/doctor.rs** — new printed section `Learning` (the :38-169 section pattern) with a `check_learn(cfg) -> Vec<Finding>` living in doctor.rs (it reads state dir + $HOME hook files, which the checks.rs purity contract forbids). Findings: enabled-but-not-activated here ("run `load learn on` on this machine"); activated-but-disabled / hooks-registered-but-disabled orphan ("run `load learn off` here to clean up" — Decision #4); hooks well-formed per agent (parse both dotfiles, locate our entries by suffix, flag malformed JSON); last run + outcome + next eligibility from log/stamps; paused-after-failures with the clearing action; corrupt watermarks (loud, names `load learn reset`); no-trigger-in-14-days nudge when enabled. Doctor NEVER triggers a harvest.

**Modify src/commands/refresh.rs** — alongside the `checks::refresh_subset` warnings (:68-72), a direct cheap check: when learning is enabled+activated and `paused_at`, print `learning paused after repeated failures — see load learn status` (stderr `warn_user!`).

**Steps:** (1) failing tests: fixture state dirs produce each finding (enabled-no-ack, orphaned hooks, paused, corrupt marks, stale 14-day); refresh pause warning appears/absent. Doctor findings tested at the `check_learn` fn level with injected paths (follow `check_hook_registry`'s testing style, doctor.rs:354-411). (2) Implement. (3) Green. (4) Commit `feat(learn): doctor learning section + refresh pause warning`.
acceptance: every design-named finding has a check and a clearing action in its message
orphaned learn hooks are flagged; doctor never spawns a worker

phases.title: Hardening + ship
phases.summary_md: End-to-end proof with a stub CLI, then the release paperwork the v0.13 retro flagged.

tasks.title: End-to-end integration suite (stub agent CLI)
tasks.summary_md:
**Create tests/learn.rs** using the `Fixture` pattern (tests/cli.rs:13 — copy the struct or extract a shared helper module if trivial) + the `agent_stub` fake-binary pattern (:873). Stub `claude` script: dumps argv+env to a file, increments a call-count file, emits canned wrapped JSON (`{"result": "{\"candidates\": […]}"}`), honors `--output-format json`.

Scenarios (each its own #[test], fully isolated HOME/CONFIG/STATE):
1. **Full cycle:** fake `~/.claude/projects/<proj>/session.jsonl` (interactive shape, old mtime) ⇒ `load learn on --yes` ⇒ `load harvest` ⇒ assert journal events + evidence file + log entry + advanced watermark + candidate visible in `load learn status`.
2. **No-new-content:** immediate second `load harvest` ⇒ stub call-count unchanged, no-op logged.
3. **Throttle:** ambient path (`load harvest --ambient`) with fresh spend stamp ⇒ zero calls; stale stamp ⇒ one call.
4. **Crash-after-stamp:** stub exits 1 after being called ⇒ failure logged, watermark unmoved, spend stamp written (tick burnt), second consecutive failure ⇒ status reports paused and ambient refuses.
5. **Recursion guard:** stub invokes `load hook claude --event session-end` itself ⇒ no second worker (LOADOUT_LEARN_WORKER env + lock Busy path both asserted via call-count).
6. **Reclaim:** pre-seed a stale lock file ⇒ run succeeds, reclaim counted as one failure in failures.json, logged.
7. **Kill switch:** `LOADOUT_LEARN=off` ⇒ ambient no-op; `load learn off` ⇒ hooks gone from both dotfiles, enabled=false.
8. **PATH-not-alias:** selection uses PATH lookup (stub dir prepended) — a shell alias can't shadow it (the dogfood note: `claude` is aliased to `load run claude` on the dev machine).

**Gate:** `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test` (ALL suites), `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo clippy --all-targets` clean, `cargo fmt --check` clean. Fix fallout. Commit `test(learn): end-to-end harvest suite with stub agent CLI`.
acceptance: all eight scenarios pass in isolation and under `cargo test` full-suite
clippy --all-targets and fmt --check are clean

tasks.title: CHANGELOG + README learning section
tasks.summary_md:
The v0.13 retro gap, made a task this time. **CHANGELOG.md:** add the `0.15.0` section (match the existing sections' voice and structure — check how 0.13.0/0.14.0 were written, and RELEASING.md for whether the header carries a date at release time): the learning feature (one-time opt-in, session-end hooks for claude/cursor, throttled ≤4-calls/day-per-machine worker, per-machine inbox journals, studio Inbox tab, `load learn`/`load harvest`, doctor section), the honest-spend guarantees, and the explicit non-goals (gemini hooks inert — tier-2; audit layer → v0.16). **README.md:** a short problem-first "Learning" section in the existing feature-list style (opt-in, mines YOUR sessions with YOUR agent CLI, everything staged for explicit promote — own-your-markdown intact); note the studio screenshot regeneration is deferred to the release step (the docs/branding pipeline exists). Version bump itself happens in the release commit per RELEASING.md, not here.

**Steps:** (1) write both; (2) `load plan`-style sanity: read them back for voice; (3) commit `docs: changelog + README section for learning (v0.15.0)`.
acceptance: CHANGELOG 0.15.0 section exists and matches house style; README explains the feature problem-first

risks.title: Transcript store formats are undocumented internals (readers/*.rs)
risks.mitigation_md: The highest ongoing maintenance surface, accepted in the design. Mitigations: per-store fail-closed readers (a parse failure skips that store with a log line, never aborts the run); fixtures cut from REAL files on this machine at implementation time (t-reader-codex explicitly re-verifies the rollout message shape before coding); formats re-verified each release.

risks.title: Suspend/resume interleavings around the spend stamp (learn/lock.rs, learn/worker.rs)
risks.mitigation_md: Token minted at acquisition; EVERY side effect (both stamps, the CLI spawn, journal/evidence/watermark/log writes) re-checks `still_held()` first; watermark advance is additionally monotonic so even a fencing bug cannot double-harvest. t-worker's tests include a fenced-out-before-spawn scenario.

risks.title: Cursor `stop` hook payload unverified at plan time (t-hook-serve-event)
risks.mitigation_md: The brainstorm verified the surface exists; the payload shape didn't get re-verified. t-hook-serve-event verifies against Cursor's docs before wiring; a missing session id degrades to a generic wake hint; a missing event entirely drops cursor to tier-2 (like gemini) with entry-point triggers covering its users. No guessing.

risks.title: Snapshot discovery line could churn the rendered header (src/render/header.rs)
risks.mitigation_md: The pending-count line must not feed the context hash. The header already varies (host, timestamp) without affecting the hash, so the line rides the same channel; t-entrypoints has an explicit test that two renders differing only in pending-count produce identical `context sha` values.

risks.title: Claude empty-toolset flag semantics unverified (learn/agent_cli.rs)
risks.mitigation_md: `--allowed-tools`/`--disallowed-tools` spellings are verified; whether `--disallowed-tools "*"` or `--allowedTools ""` blocks all tools is pinned in t-agent-cli with one real-binary run. Failure mode is mild anyway: `-p --bare` with a read-only prompt and a 5-minute deadline.

risks.title: Metered-API spend surprises (t-learn-cmd consent, t-worker stamps)
risks.mitigation_md: Stamp-before-spend ordering caps crash-loop cost at 1 call per 6h per machine; consent names CLI, model, ceiling, and a concrete per-run cost estimate computed against current pricing at implementation; per-run usage logged when the CLI reports it; 2 consecutive failures pause ambient triggering.

open_questions.question_md: `load harvest` (manual) bypasses the 6h interval check but still writes the spend stamp when it makes its call — i.e. a manual run resets the ambient tick. That matches the consent wording ("plus any `load harvest` runs you type") and is the cheapest honest semantics. Confirm, or should manual runs leave the ambient tick untouched?

At `load learn on`, the offered first run executes FOREGROUND with the run's outcome printed (recommended — the user just consented and wants to see something happen), rather than detached like ambient runs. OK?

Default cheap models when `learn.model` is unset: claude → the CLI's `haiku` alias; gemini → a flash-class id; codex → its configured default. Exact strings get pinned in t-agent-cli against each CLI's live model list (not memory). Any preference to hard-pin specific ids instead?
